timeline.notes: 82-year-old male transferred from nursing home with altered mental status, hypotension, and respiratory distress. Found down this morning, unresponsive. CXR shows bilateral lower lobe infiltrates. Meeting criteria for septic shock with end-organ dysfunction. Intubated in ED. Requires vasopressor support.

Patient deteriorating despite broad-spectrum antibiotics and fluid resuscitation. Requiring high-dose norepinephrine. Developing acute kidney injury. Lactate rising concerning for inadequate tissue perfusion. Blood cultures pending. Procalcitonin elevated at 15.2.

CRITICAL: Multi-organ failure developing. Refractory hypotension despite maximum vasopressor support. Acute kidney injury progressing. DIC suspected with dropping platelets. Family meeting held regarding goals of care. Considering CRRT for renal replacement.

Severe septic shock with refractory hypotension. Blood cultures positive for Streptococcus pneumoniae. Switched to targeted antibiotic therapy. Added vasopressin. Patient extremely critical with poor prognosis. Discussing comfort care measures with family.

Patient in extremis with impending cardiovascular collapse. Bradycardia and hypothermia concerning for end-stage shock. Lactic acidosis severe. Family has chosen comfort care. Discontinuing aggressive interventions. Transitioning to palliative care measures.
